issue.title: Add Gateway 2.0 ("thin client") support to the Rust Cosmos driver and SDK
issue.body: Spec authored in PR #4223 (sdk/cosmos/azure_data_cosmos_driver/docs/GATEWAY_20_SPEC.md). This artifact mirrors that spec into the harness schema so the orchestrator can drive Phase 3 (Implementation) → Phase 6 (Testing) without re-running Phase 2 (Spec Generation).

analysis.problem_statement: The Rust Cosmos SDK only supports Gateway V1 today, which has no latency SLA. Java and .NET both ship a Gateway 2.0 ("thin client") path: RNTBD message encoding over HTTP/2 to a regional proxy that owns replica selection. Rust needs the same surface so customers can get direct-mode-class latency without taking on direct-mode COGS/debug burden.
analysis.root_cause: Gateway 2.0 was never implemented for Rust. There is no RNTBD encoder/decoder, no Gateway-2.0-specific header injection, no operation-eligibility check, and no auto-detection from account metadata.

related_files.relevance: Transport pipeline root; new rntbd module is added under transport/.
decide_throttle_action lives here; new ThrottleAction::RetryWith branch added for 449.
x-ms-cosmos-sdk-supportedcapabilities header value must move from "0" to (PartitionMerge | IgnoreUnknownRntbdTokens) bitmask.
Operation pipeline; Gateway 2.0 retry classification and PLF precedence live here.
ThrottleAction enum; RetryWith variant introduced here.
EffectivePartitionKey::compute() / compute_range() are the canonical EPK source for Gateway 2.0 header injection.
SDK-side EPK call site that must be cut over from get_hashed_partition_key_string to the driver's EffectivePartitionKey.
THINCLIENT_PROXY_* constants are removed here and relocated/renamed to GATEWAY20_* in the driver.
Authoritative spec — every requirement and AC traces back to a section here.
Sharded HTTP/2 transport contract; Gateway 2.0 reuses the existing (HttpClient, host:port) shard key verbatim.
PKRange cache; Gateway 2.0 must NOT refresh on 404/1002 (READ_SESSION_NOT_AVAILABLE).
PLF takes precedence over the Gateway 2.0 prefer-remote-region hint on 404/1002 retry.

analysis.existing_patterns: The driver already wires a sharded HTTP/2 transport (TRANSPORT_PIPELINE_SPEC), a 7-stage operation pipeline, an EffectivePartitionKey newtype with compute()/compute_range(), and a CosmosClientOptions surface. The SDK already emits x-ms-cosmos-sdk-supportedcapabilities (currently "0"). The retry pipeline already differentiates 408/410/429/503; 449 currently falls through to the generic throttle path. PLF is already implemented per its own spec. Gateway 2.0 layers cleanly onto these patterns — no new transport, no new pipeline, no new EPK type.

spec.objective: Add a Gateway 2.0 transport path (RNTBD-over-HTTP/2 to regional proxy) to the Rust Cosmos driver, auto-enabled when account metadata advertises thin-client endpoints, with operator opt-out via CosmosClientOptions::gateway20_disabled and full retry / diagnostics / test parity with Java and .NET.

requirements.description: Phase 1 — Implement RNTBD wire format under azure_data_cosmos_driver::driver::transport::rntbd (mod.rs, request.rs, response.rs, tokens.rs, status.rs). Request frame: uint32 LE total length (inclusive of itself), uint16 LE resource type, uint16 LE operation type, 16-byte activity ID written as two uint64 LE (MSB then LSB — NOT RFC 4122 byte order). Response frame: uint32 LE length, uint32 LE status, 16-byte activity ID, metadata token stream, optional body.

RNTBD response deserializer MUST silently skip unknown token type IDs (consume `length` bytes and continue). MUST NOT panic, error, log per-token, or drop the rest of the response. This is the SDK side of the IgnoreUnknownRntbdTokens capability bit.

Change x-ms-cosmos-sdk-supportedcapabilities (COSMOS_SDK_SUPPORTEDCAPABILITIES) value emitted on every gateway request from "0" to the bitmask string for (PartitionMerge | IgnoreUnknownRntbdTokens). Pin the integer value against .NET's SDKSupportedCapabilities.cs and commit as a Rust constant alongside the existing header-name constant.

Phase 2 — Add is_operation_supported_by_gateway20(resource_type, operation_type) -> bool. Only ResourceType::Document is eligible. StoredProcedure Execute is explicitly excluded (out of scope for Rust GA). All other resource types fall through to standard gateway via the eligibility-fallback path.

Route EPK computation through the driver's EffectivePartitionKey::compute() (point ops) / ::compute_range() (feed/cross-partition ops). Cut over SDK-side callers of azure_data_cosmos::hash::get_hashed_partition_key_string. EPK errors (MultiHash V1, component-count mismatch) surface as CosmosStatus::BadRequest — do NOT fall back to standard gateway.

Inject Gateway 2.0 HTTP/2 request headers when transport_mode == Gateway20: x-ms-thinclient-proxy-operation-type, x-ms-thinclient-proxy-resource-type, x-ms-effective-partition-key (point ops), x-ms-documentdb-partitionkey (point ops + single-logical-partition query), x-ms-thinclient-range-min / x-ms-thinclient-range-max (feed/cross-partition), x-ms-cosmos-use-thinclient (account metadata fetch only). Wire-header strings stay as x-ms-thinclient-*; Rust identifiers use the GATEWAY20_* prefix.

Emit the GlobalDatabaseAccountName RNTBD token (0x00CE, String) in the metadata stream of every Gateway 2.0 request (point, feed, batch, bulk, change feed). Value is the host-label of the account endpoint URL (e.g. "myacct" from myacct.documents.azure.com), parsed once at client construction.

Implement the ConsistencyLevel ↔ ReadConsistencyStrategy reconciliation per §3 of the spec. Single resolution step in the driver pipeline runs BEFORE transport selection. Resolution precedence: Request-RCS > Request-CL > Client-RCS > Client-CL > account default. RCS::Default is a pass-through. Writes skip RCS sources. RCS=GlobalStrong on a non-Strong account is a fail-fast azure_core::Error (no wire request emitted).

Wire carriers for the resolved consistency value: V1 HTTP -> x-ms-cosmos-read-consistency-strategy (case-sensitive strings: Eventual / Session / LatestCommitted / GlobalStrong; header omitted when resolved is Default). V2 RNTBD -> token 0x00F0 Byte (Eventual=0x01, Session=0x02, LatestCommitted=0x03, GlobalStrong=0x04; token omitted when resolved is Default). MUST be Byte-encoded — String-typed earlier prototype hung the proxy.

Enforce mutual exclusion between x-ms-consistency-level and x-ms-cosmos-read-consistency-strategy on V1 HTTP, and between the ConsistencyLevel and ReadConsistencyStrategy RNTBD tokens on V2. Strip the loser; never emit both on either transport.
Resolution must NOT mutate the operation's original request headers (header-map immutability). An applySessionToken-equivalent rewrite path must not be able to clobber x-ms-consistency-level.

Phase 4 — 449 (Retry-With) is a NEW dedicated ThrottleAction variant: ThrottleAction::RetryWith { delay, new_state: RetryWithState }. Lives in driver/pipeline/components.rs alongside the existing Retry / Propagate variants. decide_throttle_action in driver/transport/transport_pipeline.rs emits it whenever (status_code, sub_status) == (449, *). RetryWithState owns the 449 retry budget, distinct from ThrottleRetryState — 449 retries CANNOT consume 410/Gone or 429 budget, and vice versa. Policy: ≤3 attempts, exponential backoff, same endpoint, no region switch, no fallback to Gateway V1.

404 + sub-status 1002 (READ_SESSION_NOT_AVAILABLE) retry: prefer a remote region from the client's preferred-region list. Only retry the local region when no other region is available. PLF takes precedence — when PLF has pinned a region for this PKRangeId, the PLF region wins. Do NOT refresh the PKRange cache on 404/1002 (this is the only Gateway-2.0-specific deviation from the standard 404/READ_SESSION_NOT_AVAILABLE path).

Phase 5 — Auto-enable Gateway 2.0 when account metadata advertises thinClientReadableLocations / thinClientWritableLocations. CosmosEndpoint gets gateway20_url; resolve_endpoint() prefers Gateway 2.0 when !gateway20_suppressed (= !options.gateway20_disabled && account.has_thin_client_endpoints()). Routing decision is made ONCE per logical operation in resolve_endpoint and inherited by all retries / sub-requests via the operation context.

Add CosmosClientOptions::gateway20_disabled (default false) as the single supported per-client opt-out. Negative-term name only — no positive-term internal flag (no enable_gateway20 / is_gateway20_allowed / etc.) anywhere. Not exposed via env var. Public docstring MUST carry the warning that flipping it voids Gateway 2.0's latency SLA and impacts 24/7 Microsoft support eligibility for performance regressions.

Relocate THINCLIENT_PROXY_* constants from azure_data_cosmos::constants into azure_data_cosmos_driver::constants and rename to the GATEWAY20_* family. Wire-header strings unchanged (server-defined). No SDK re-export — the SDK calls the generic CosmosDriver::execute_operation interface and the driver decides Gateway 2.0 vs V1 internally.

PLF interaction: when PLF selects a region, if that region's CosmosEndpoint exposes gateway20_url, the request uses Gateway 2.0; otherwise it falls back to standard gateway for that partition until PLF releases its override. PLF chooses the region; Gateway 2.0 is preferred whenever it is available in that region.
Diagnostics: CosmosDiagnostics MUST report whether each request used Gateway 2.0 vs standard gateway via TransportKind::Gateway20.
Update the SDK user agent string to indicate Gateway 2.0 capability.

Use HTTP/2 with prior knowledge on the Gateway 2.0 transport (no ALPN fallback to HTTP/1.x). The broader ALPN default in TRANSPORT_PIPELINE_SPEC.md does NOT apply to Gateway 2.0; if HTTP/2 negotiation fails, the request fails and existing retry policies handle it.

Phase 6 — Add a new dedicated CI pipeline sdk/cosmos/ci-gateway20.yml for Gateway 2.0 live tests. Trigger: PR changes to sdk/cosmos/** + manual dispatch. Uses a dedicated, pre-provisioned multi-region Cosmos account (NOT Bicep-provisioned per run); credentials in pipeline secrets (AZURE_COSMOS_GW20_ENDPOINT, AZURE_COSMOS_GW20_KEY). Verify thinClientReadableLocations in account metadata at pipeline start. Test matrix covers single-region, multi-region failover, eligibility fallback, operator override.

Phase 6 — Add unit + integration + E2E test files: tests/gateway20_rntbd_tests.rs, tests/gateway20_pipeline_tests.rs, tests/emulator_tests/gateway20_e2e.rs. Edit tests/emulator_tests/cosmos_fault_injection.rs to add Gateway 2.0 fault scenarios. Coverage matrix per §6 of the spec is the contract — every row is a must.

Naming: use "Gateway 2.0" everywhere in Rust code, docs, and comments. Reserve "thin client" only for (a) referencing Java/.NET source symbols and (b) literal wire-header strings containing thinclient. The eligibility helper is is_operation_supported_by_gateway20() — do NOT reintroduce is_supported_by_gw20().

acceptance_criteria.description: RNTBD round-trip: request frame and response frame serialize/deserialize losslessly across all eligible Document operations (Create/Read/Replace/Upsert/Delete/Patch/Query/QueryPlan/ReadFeed/Batch/Bulk).

Activity ID encoded as MSB-then-LSB pair of uint64 LE (NOT RFC 4122) — pinned with the worked example UUID 0a1b2c3d-4e5f-6789-abcd-ef0123456789 → MSB bytes 89 67 5f 4e 3d 2c 1b 0a, LSB bytes 89 67 45 23 01 ef cd ab.
Synthetic unknown RNTBD token type ID injected into a hand-crafted response frame round-trips without error; every recognized token surfaces correctly; no panic, no error, no per-token log.

Every gateway request (V1 and Gateway 2.0) carries the x-ms-cosmos-sdk-supportedcapabilities header with the bitmask integer string for (PartitionMerge | IgnoreUnknownRntbdTokens), NOT "0". Pinned against .NET SDKSupportedCapabilities.cs.

is_operation_supported_by_gateway20 returns true for ResourceType::Document × {Create, Read, Replace, Upsert, Delete, Patch, Query, QueryPlan, ReadFeed (LatestVersion), Batch, Bulk}; returns false for StoredProcedure Execute and every non-Document resource type.

Hierarchical container, full PK on a point op → emits x-ms-effective-partition-key from EffectivePartitionKey::compute(). Hierarchical container, partial PK on a feed/cross-partition/delete-by-PK op → emits x-ms-thinclient-range-min / x-ms-thinclient-range-max from EffectivePartitionKey::compute_range(). Verified at unit (header presence + exact wire form) and E2E (round-trip against a live HPK container).

GlobalDatabaseAccountName RNTBD token (0x00CE, String) appears in the metadata stream of every Gateway 2.0 request (point, feed, batch, bulk, change feed). Value matches the host label of the account endpoint URL.

Consistency reconciliation: token 0x00F0 Byte round-trips for all 4 strategies (Eventual=0x01, Session=0x02, LatestCommitted=0x03, GlobalStrong=0x04) on V2; HTTP header x-ms-cosmos-read-consistency-strategy uses the exact case-sensitive wire strings on V1; Default emits NEITHER carrier on EITHER transport.

Across all 16 (CL × RCS, request-level × client-level) combinations, the SDK never emits both x-ms-consistency-level AND x-ms-cosmos-read-consistency-strategy on V1, and never emits both ConsistencyLevel and ReadConsistencyStrategy RNTBD tokens on V2.

RCS resolution honors the 4-source precedence (Request-RCS > Request-CL > Client-RCS > Client-CL > account default). Default at any RCS layer is a pass-through. Write op + RCS set → RCS ignored, ConsistencyLevel flows through.
RCS=GlobalStrong on a non-Strong account → fail-fast azure_core::Error with no wire request emitted. RCS=GlobalStrong on a Strong account → request proceeds.
Header-map immutability: resolution does not mutate the operation's original request headers. An applySessionToken-equivalent rewrite cannot clobber x-ms-consistency-level.

ThrottleAction::RetryWith { delay, new_state: RetryWithState } variant exists in driver/pipeline/components.rs. decide_throttle_action emits it for any (449, *). 449 retry: ≤3 attempts, exponential backoff, same Gateway 2.0 endpoint, no region switch, no fallback to V1. RetryWithState counters are structurally distinct from ThrottleRetryState.

404/1002 retry: routes to a remote-preferred region when one is available (assert local-region retry only when no other region is available). When PLF has pinned a region for this PKRangeId, the PLF region wins. PKRange cache is NOT refreshed.

Auto-detection: when account metadata includes thinClientReadableLocations / thinClientWritableLocations, eligible Document ops route through Gateway 2.0 with no user opt-in. Default resolve_endpoint() picks Gateway 2.0 when !gateway20_suppressed.

Operator override: CosmosClientOptions::gateway20_disabled = true → ALL eligible Document ops (point + feed + batch + change feed) route through standard gateway. Default false → behavior unchanged. No env var toggles Gateway 2.0.

Constants placement: GATEWAY20_OPERATION_TYPE / GATEWAY20_RESOURCE_TYPE / GATEWAY20_RANGE_MIN / GATEWAY20_RANGE_MAX / GATEWAY20_USE_THINCLIENT / EFFECTIVE_PARTITION_KEY all live in azure_data_cosmos_driver::constants. The old THINCLIENT_PROXY_* constants are removed from azure_data_cosmos::constants. No SDK re-export.
PLF interaction: PLF-selected region with gateway20_url uses Gateway 2.0; PLF-selected region without gateway20_url falls back to standard gateway for that partition until PLF releases.

Diagnostics: CosmosDiagnostics output reports TransportKind::Gateway20 for Gateway 2.0 requests and the corresponding standard-gateway kind for V1 requests. Verified at both unit and integration level.

Gateway 2.0 transport uses HTTP/2 with prior knowledge (no ALPN fallback to HTTP/1.x). HTTP/2 negotiation failure surfaces as a transport error handled by existing retry policies, not as a silent downgrade.

Routing decision is made once per logical operation in resolve_endpoint and inherited by all retries / sub-requests via the operation context. Downstream stages MUST NOT re-derive eligibility mid-operation.

sdk/cosmos/ci-gateway20.yml exists and runs on PR changes to sdk/cosmos/** + manual dispatch. Pipeline reads AZURE_COSMOS_GW20_ENDPOINT and AZURE_COSMOS_GW20_KEY from secrets, verifies thinClientReadableLocations at startup, runs the §6 test matrix, and produces JUnit XML + diagnostics + perf metrics.

All Phase 6 test files exist (tests/gateway20_rntbd_tests.rs, tests/gateway20_pipeline_tests.rs, tests/emulator_tests/gateway20_e2e.rs) and tests/emulator_tests/cosmos_fault_injection.rs has Gateway 2.0 scenarios. The §6 coverage matrix is the contract — every row maps to at least one passing test.

Naming audit: ripgrep finds zero occurrences of is_supported_by_gw20, enable_gateway20, is_gateway20_allowed, or any positive-term Gateway 2.0 flag anywhere in the repo. "thin client" appears only in (a) Java/.NET symbol references in comments / spec text and (b) wire-header strings literally containing thinclient.

spec.technical_approach: Implement the spec phase-by-phase per §5 of GATEWAY_20_SPEC.md. Phase 1 (RNTBD protocol) and the verification parts of Phase 3 (endpoint discovery) can run in parallel. Phase 2 (request pipeline) depends on Phase 1 and folds in Phase 3's one new header (x-ms-cosmos-use-thinclient). Phases 4 (retry) and 5 (SDK integration) depend on Phase 2. Phase 6 (testing) depends on all previous phases. Reuse the existing sharded HTTP/2 transport (TRANSPORT_PIPELINE_SPEC.md) verbatim — do NOT introduce a new transport layer or a Gateway-2.0-specific timeout regime. Reuse the existing ClientRetryPolicy chain — Gateway 2.0 introduces exactly two retry-policy changes: (1) ThrottleAction::RetryWith for 449, (2) prefer-remote-region + no-PKRange-refresh on 404/1002. Reuse EffectivePartitionKey from the driver crate as the single source of truth for EPK — do NOT introduce a new EPK type, and do NOT consume the SDK-side hash::get_hashed_partition_key_string. SDK has no Gateway-2.0 surface area; the driver decides Gateway 2.0 vs V1 internally.
spec.test_strategy: Layered per the §6 test coverage matrix in GATEWAY_20_SPEC.md. Unit tests in the driver crate cover RNTBD serialization, RNTBD unknown-token tolerance, EPK computation, operation filtering, header injection, account-name token, supported-capabilities header, full consistency reconciliation suite (token + header encoding, dual-header rejection, 4-source precedence, GlobalStrong validation, header-map immutability, write-op behavior). Integration tests cover Gateway 2.0 transport wiring, read/write pairing, retry policies (408 / 449 / 503 / 410 Gone / 404+1002), operator override, eligibility fallback, PLF precedence, fault injection, diagnostics validation. E2E tests (require provisioned account in the new Gateway 2.0 CI pipeline) cover point CRUD, query (single + cross-partition), batch, bulk, change feed (LatestVersion), HPK + Gateway 2.0 (full vs partial PK), multi-region failover, perf benchmarks. The 449 RetryWithState budget separation is a structural unit test (the type system itself prevents 449 from sharing budget with 410 / 429). Test Planner sub-agent should mine Java's ThinClientE2ETest and .NET's CosmosItemThinClientTests for cross-SDK test scenarios per the Test Planner schema.
risks: Activity-ID byte order is non-RFC-4122. Easy to get wrong; pin with the spec's worked-example test (AC2).
SDK-supportedcapabilities header value is a magic integer; pin against .NET source and add a regression test (AC4).
RNTBD token 0x00F0 must be Byte-encoded — String-typed earlier prototype hung the proxy. Lock byte values against the proxy's C++ enum (AC8).
Dual-header / dual-token mutual exclusion is easy to regress when new code paths add headers. Coverage must be all 16 (CL × RCS) combinations across both transports (AC9).

Routing decision must be made once per logical operation. A retry that re-derives gateway20_suppressed could route through a different transport than the original attempt and break session-token affinity (AC21).
Operator override (gateway20_disabled) must be negative-term only. A positive-term flag slipping in anywhere (driver, SDK, perf, env var, test wiring) defeats the spec's safety story (AC24).
EPK error path must NOT fall back to standard gateway. The same inputs would be equally broken there. Surface as CosmosStatus::BadRequest.
PLF precedence on 404/1002 retry interacts with the prefer-remote-region hint. PLF wins — easy to get wrong if both code paths run independently (AC14).
PKRange cache must NOT be refreshed on 404/1002 (Gateway-2.0-specific deviation). Easy to regress if a future change unifies the 404 retry path with 410/Gone.

Live-test pipeline depends on a hardcoded pre-provisioned account. If the account is deleted or its endpoints change, the pipeline silently regresses to non-Gateway-2.0 coverage. Mitigation: verify thinClientReadableLocations at pipeline startup.

alternatives_considered.approach: Hand-write a separate Gateway-2.0 transport stack alongside the existing sharded HTTP/2 transport.
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Doubles the timeout/hedging/shard-key surface area for no benefit. The existing transport (TRANSPORT_PIPELINE_SPEC.md) already supports HTTP/2 with prior knowledge; reusing it keeps the diagnostics, retry, and timeout regime uniform across V1 and Gateway 2.0.

alternatives_considered.approach: Introduce a Gateway-2.0-specific failure-fallback state machine (per-partition consecutive-failure counter, sticky standard-gateway state, cooldown).
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Java's ThinClientStoreModel takes the same posture — model selection is per-request and stateless via useThinClientStoreModel(); the existing ClientRetryPolicy / WebExceptionRetryPolicy chain handles transport errors and 502/503/504 uniformly. A Rust-only state machine would fragment retry telemetry and create a behavior gap with Java/.NET.

alternatives_considered.approach: Add a positive-term flag (enable_gateway20 / is_gateway20_allowed) to make the opt-in path explicit.
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Default-on with operator opt-out (gateway20_disabled) matches Java and .NET and minimizes friction for the common case. A positive-term flag would bias users toward keeping it off and missing the latency-SLA benefit; it also fragments the on/off semantics across two negation levels.

alternatives_considered.approach: Reuse ThrottleAction::Retry (the existing variant) for 449 with a flag indicating Retry-With semantics.
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Conflates 449's separate retry budget with ThrottleRetryState's 410/429 budgets. A flag-on-the-existing-variant lets a future code path accidentally consume the wrong budget. A new ThrottleAction::RetryWith { delay, new_state: RetryWithState } variant makes the budget separation structural — the type system enforces it.

alternatives_considered.approach: Introduce a new EPK-range struct on the driver-crate Gateway 2.0 path.
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: EPK range types are already canonical in the driver crate (azure_data_cosmos_driver::models::range::EpkRange<T>, EffectivePartitionKey::compute_range() returning std::ops::Range<EffectivePartitionKey>). A parallel struct would fork the EPK contract and create silent serialization mismatches between Gateway 2.0 and standard gateway.

alternatives_considered.approach: Send both x-ms-consistency-level and x-ms-cosmos-read-consistency-strategy when both are resolved, letting the proxy pick.
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: The compute gateway explicitly rejects requests carrying both. Mutual exclusion must be enforced client-side (AC9). Sending both is a client bug.

alternatives_considered.approach: Use an environment variable to toggle Gateway 2.0 (e.g. AZURE_COSMOS_GATEWAY20_DISABLED).
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Env-var toggles enable casual / fleet-wide enablement and bypass code review. The spec is explicit: gateway20_disabled is per-client only, set via CosmosClientOptions, with a public docstring carrying the SLA-voiding warning. No env var.

alternatives_considered.approach: Provision the Gateway 2.0 live test account per pipeline run via Bicep.
alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Cosmos DB account configuration flags required to enable Gateway 2.0 endpoints are not part of the standard Bicep templates (Q2 in §6 of the spec). A hardcoded, pre-provisioned account is the only viable path until Bicep gains those flags.

risk_assessment.performance_impact: positive — Gateway 2.0 provides a contractual latency SLA Gateway V1 lacks; eligible Document ops auto-route to the SLA path when the account advertises Gateway 2.0 endpoints.
test_coverage_needed: RNTBD round-trip (request + response, all eligible Document ops)
RNTBD activity-ID byte-order pin (worked-example UUID)
RNTBD unknown-token silent-skip (synthetic unrecognized token ID)
x-ms-cosmos-sdk-supportedcapabilities bitmask string (NOT "0"; pinned vs .NET)
is_operation_supported_by_gateway20 — full ResourceType × OperationType matrix (incl. SPROC Execute = false)
EPK compute() / compute_range() routing (cut over from get_hashed_partition_key_string)
EPK error → CosmosStatus::BadRequest (NO fallback to standard gateway)
Gateway 2.0 HTTP/2 header injection (point vs feed)
GlobalDatabaseAccountName RNTBD token (0x00CE) on every Gateway 2.0 request
HPK + Gateway 2.0: full PK → x-ms-effective-partition-key; partial PK → range-min/range-max
Consistency token 0x00F0 Byte round-trip (4 strategies)
Consistency HTTP header exact wire-string mapping (4 strategies)
Default emits NEITHER carrier on EITHER transport
Dual-header / dual-token rejection (16 combinations)
RCS 4-source precedence (representative subset)
RCS=GlobalStrong on non-Strong account → fail-fast
Header-map immutability under resolution
Write op + RCS set → RCS ignored, CL flows through
ThrottleAction::RetryWith for 449 (≤3 attempts, exponential backoff, same endpoint, no region switch, no V1 fallback)
RetryWithState budget structurally separate from ThrottleRetryState (449 + 410 + 429 do not share counters)
404/1002 retry: prefer-remote-region (assert local-only when no other region exists)
404/1002 retry: PLF precedence (PLF region wins when pinned)
404/1002 retry: NO PKRange cache refresh
Auto-detection: thinClientReadableLocations triggers Gateway 2.0 routing without user opt-in
Operator override: gateway20_disabled = true routes ALL eligible Document ops via standard gateway
PLF precedence: PLF region with gateway20_url → Gateway 2.0; without → standard gateway
Routing decision once per logical operation; retries inherit (no re-derivation)
Diagnostics: TransportKind::Gateway20 in CosmosDiagnostics output
HTTP/2 prior knowledge: no ALPN fallback to HTTP/1.x
Naming audit: zero positive-term flags; "thin client" only in Java/.NET refs and wire strings
Live tests pipeline: ci-gateway20.yml runs on sdk/cosmos/** PR changes; verifies thinClientReadableLocations at startup; emits JUnit + diagnostics + perf

_seed_notes.instructions_for_phase_3: Treat this spec.json as authoritative, but ALWAYS read GATEWAY_20_SPEC.md for the full normative text (especially §3.1 gating formula, §4 retry behavior, RNTBD wire-format tables, header naming table, and the §6 test coverage matrix). The acceptance criteria in this artifact are the must-pass list; the §6 coverage matrix in the spec doc is the contract.
_seed_notes.implementation_phase_dependency_graph: Phase 1 (RNTBD) and verification parts of Phase 3 (endpoint discovery) can run in parallel. Phase 2 (request pipeline) depends on Phase 1 and folds in Phase 3's new header. Phases 4 (retry) and 5 (SDK integration) depend on Phase 2. Phase 6 (testing) depends on all previous phases.
